channel.title: The Trading Journal
channel.description: A daily log of trades, lessons, and probabilities

item.title: XSP Long Call Condor — Dec 18 '26 690/700/810/820: 5-Month Range-Bound Lower-Vol Structure
item.description: Opened a 110-wide XSP long call condor (Dec 18 '26, 690/700/810/820), short the body's IV skew while harvesting theta into year-end. $609 defined risk; max profit $10,391 between $700–$810.

item.title: SPX Bull Put Spread — Thursday, July 23, 2026
item.description: SPX closed Wed at $7,498.96. Bull regime intact. SPX Aug 21 AM-settled 7200/7180 20-wide bull put, $360 credit, $1,640 max loss, ~80% POP, FOMC 5 days out.

item.title: NASA Bull Call Spread — Jan 21 '28 30/15: Long-Dated Space-ETF Upside
item.description: Opened a NASA ETF Jan 21 2028 30/15 bull call spread, an 18-month LEAPS upside structure on the Tema Space Innovators ETF. Net debit $6.10, max loss $610/contract × 1 = $610 total. Cushion to long strike 36%, breakeven $21.10.

item.title: RSP Long Call Condor — Jan 21 '28 230/220/290/280: Equal-Weight SPY Trade on a Flat Tape
item.description: Opened an RSP ETF Jan 21 2028 230/220/290/280 long call condor, an 18-month structure expressing the view that the equal-weight S&P 500 will consolidate in a $63-wide profit zone without a major breakout. Net debit $3.175/share, $317.50/contract × 1 = $317.50 total, 1:2.15 risk:reward.

item.title: RSP Short Upper Condor — Jan 21 '28 220/230/280/285: Same Debit, Free Upside Above $287
item.description: Opened a second RSP ETF Jan 21 2028 220/230/280/285 long call condor with a $5-wide upper body (vs $10-wide on RSP #1). Same $3.175/share net debit but a permanent $182.50/contract upside plateau above $287 instead of a $317.50 max loss above $290. Direct comparison to RSP #1 (mBisziPFxRvo) shipped earlier today.

item.title: SPX Bull Put Spread — Wednesday, July 22, 2026
item.description: SPX closed Tuesday at $7,509.20. Bull regime intact (20d +0.5%, breadth 75%, IV-rank 42.5). SPX Aug 21 AM-settled 7235/7225 bull put spread, $240 credit, $760 max loss, ~80% POP, FOMC one week out.

item.title: Tuesday Pre-Market Brief — SPX Long Strangle at $7,443 Spot: $7,095 Put / $7,890 Call, Aug 21 AM-Settled Expiry
item.description: SPX closed Monday at $7,443.28. TRANSITION regime — price between MA50 and MA200, no clear trend. SPX Aug 21 AM-settled long strangle 7095/7890, $4,285 debit, ~32% POP, vol expansion play.

item.title: NASA Bull Put Spread — Jan 15 '27 25/20: Long-Dated Space-ETF Premium
item.description: Opened a NASA ETF Jan 15 2027 25/20 bull put spread, a 178-DTE long-dated short-premium structure on the Tema Space Innovators ETF. Live mid credit $2.525, max loss $247.50/contract × 4 = $990 total. ~60% short-leg POP, ~1¢/day theta.

item.title: Monday Pre-Market Brief — SPX Bull Put Spread at $7,457 Spot: $7,170 / $7,155, Aug 21 AM-Settled Expiry
item.description: SPX closed Friday at $7,457.69. BULL regime with 20d trend still positive but a mild 1.5% 5d pullback. SPX Aug 21 AM-settled 7170/7155 bull put spread, ~$300 credit, ~$1,200 max loss, ~80% POP.

item.title: Monday Pre-Market Brief: SPX Bull Put Spread at $7,457 Spot, Expiring Aug 21, 2026
item.description: SPX closed Friday at $7,457.69 with VIX 18.77, FOMC 9 days out. SPX Aug 21 (AM-settled) 7125/7135 bull put spread, ~$130 credit, ~$870 max loss, ~80% POP.

item.title: XSP Bear Call Spread — Aug 28 '26 749/750: A 1-Contract Insurance Tail
item.description: Added a 1-contract XSP Aug 28 749/750 bear call spread as defined-risk premium-collection insurance. $61.50 net credit, $38.50 defined max loss, ~50% short-leg POP.

item.title: XSP Bull Put Spread — Jul 31 '26 742/740: A Short-Dated Index Pin
item.description: Opened an XSP Jul 31 742/740 bull put spread, an 11-DTE index-level short-premium structure with 6.93-point OTM cushion. $237.50 total credit, $762.50 defined max loss, ~67% short-leg POP.

item.title: Bear Call Spread: The Compliment to the Bull Put
item.description: Anatomy of a bear call vertical credit spread: the symmetric counter to the bull put. Used as a standalone bearish/neutral expression or as the upper half of an iron condor.

item.title: Covered Call: The Income Overlay on Equity You Already Own
item.description: Anatomy of the covered call: sell a call against 100 shares you own. The simplest income strategy in options, with one structural risk that defines the whole position.

item.title: Iron Condor: The Range-Bound, IV-Rich Theta Engine
item.description: Anatomy of the iron condor: a four-leg structure that collects premium in both wings when the underlying stays inside a defined range. The highest-theta structure in the playbook, paired with the tightest management rule.

item.title: Playbook Lessons — Five Months of Trades, Six Rules Worth Testing for v1.1
item.description: Six 'For the playbook:' items captured from the trade-log since v1.0 shipped. Each maps to a candidate rule change for v1.1 — deep-OTM directional structures, LEAPS verticals, inverse diagonals, rolling diagonals, 5% pullback scale-ins, and long-dated diagonals. v1.0 remains canonical until v1.1 ships.

item.title: Long Call: The Single-Leg Directional Building Block
item.description: Anatomy of a long call: max loss capped at premium paid, infinite upside, vega-positive and theta-negative. When single-leg directional exposure fits the playbook better than a vertical.

item.title: Long Straddle: The Volatility Expression
item.description: Anatomy of the long straddle: ATM call + ATM put, same strike, same expiry. The cleanest expression of 'I do not know the direction, but I expect volatility.' Used around earnings, FOMC, and known catalysts.

item.title: USAR Bull Call Spread — Jan 21 '28 20/25: A Long-Dated US Critical-Minerals Position
item.description: Opened a USAR Jan 21 '28 20/25 bull call spread, a long-dated (553 DTE) LEAPS expressing the US critical-minerals / rare-earth processing thesis. $80 defined risk, $420 max profit (5.25:1 R/R), 29.6% cushion to lower breakeven.

item.title: SKHY Bull Call Spread — Sep 18 '26 195/200: A Memory-Cycle HBM Position
item.description: Opened an SKHY Sep 18 195/200 bull call spread, OTM speculative structure expressing a memory-cycle rally thesis. $80 defined risk, $420 max profit (5.25:1 R/R), 64 DTE.
